{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Field Pharmacist",
  "description": "INTERSOS is an independent humanitarian organization that assists the victims of natural disasters, armed conflicts and exclusion. Its activities are based on the principles of solidarity, justice, human dignity, equality of rights and opportunities, respect for diversity and coexistence, paying special attention to the most vulnerable people.

We are&nbsp;recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Field Pharmacist

Job Locations:&nbsp;Monguno and Magumeri, Borno

Job Purpose


	According to Project manager and Pharmacy Officer&#39;s indications ensure the pharmacy stock management according to the good storage and distribution practices.
	This includes receiving, storing and dispensing process for drugs, consumables, medical material and equipment.
	The pharmacist is also in charge of providing treatment precautions and recommendations to the patients.


Responsibilities
Data Collection and Analysis:


	Be able to provide at any given time reliable information on stock, consumption, use of medicines and health products for each project.
	Assist the pharmacist at central level in updating stock information when required
	Create/update the stock-cards of the drug/medical material/equipment received and other tools in a timely manner
	Ensure regular and appropriate use of the management document (inventory records, delivery notes, computer tools etc.) Update the global stock inventory
	Ensure the storage of the items according to good practices of storage
	Compile, in collaboration with the medical doctor and PM, a stock transfer request to the central pharmacy
	Work closely with the medical department to check, in relation to epidemiological data, the status of stocks and the consumptions and assess the appropriateness of prescriptions.


Supply Management:


	Ensure order reception by verifying and recording the quantity and quality of the drugs/medical materials/equipment received. Inform central pharmacy and supervisor in the event of non-compliance.
	Prepare order/supply request regarding the average monthly consumption, the stock level and supply delay in collaboration with the medical doctor.
	Validate the supply request with the Project manager and send the stock transfer request to the central pharmacy Update the global stock inventory.


Stock Management:


	Set up and maintain the pharmacy stock according to good storage practices (storage of drugs by categories, alphabetic order and expiry date).
	Ensure that FEFO (first expired first out) is respected.
	Implement and ensure the stock follow up by calculating the average monthly consumption and minimal stock level for the main items
	Implement stock management system for each item and update each stock card/follow up in a timely manner
	Ensure a physical inventory of the pharmacy and medical stock, including consumables and laboratory material on a monthly basis
	Inform the project manager in case of any missing/damaged/expired item
	Make sure that the main stock and the pharmacy are kept clean
	Organise effective destruction and quarantine of damaged items in collaboration with the Central pharmacist
	Ensure pharmacovigilance including the immediate report of any problem with the quality of a drug in accordance with INTERSOS pharmaceutical management procedures.


Treatment Delivery:


	Ensure all medical equipment is prepared for the day
	Deliver the treatment to the patient according to medical doctor prescription
	Explain the posology, and providing advices and precaution of use
	Prepare the drugs for the pharmacy on a weekly basis according to the needs


Position Requirements
Education:


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Pharmacy (B.Pharm or Pharm.D)
	Registration with Pharmacy Council of Nigeria with a current License.


Professional experience:


	Desirable previous experience in pharmacy for at least 2 years in similar or relevant positions.


Professional requirements:


	Essential computer literacy (word, excel and internet).


Personal requirements:


	Commitment to INTERSOS principles.


Languages:


	Mission language is essential.
	Local working language desirable.


Note


	Interested candidates should complete the form&nbsp;and attach their CV with Cover Letter following this format &ldquo;Surname_ Position you applied for&rdquo;.
	ONLY applicants whose competencies meet the requirements of the position will be contacted.
	Please note: Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis.
	INTERSOS reserves the right to close the advertisement before the stated closing date if a suitable candidate is identified.
	INTERSOS is committed to achieving workforce diversity in terms of gender, nationality, and culture. Individuals from minority groups, indigenous groups, and persons with disabilities are equally encouraged to apply.
	INTERSOS has a PSEA Policy that the successful candidate will be expected to comply with and promote.
",

  "validThrough": "2026-03-31",

Field Pharmacist Job at Intersos Humanitarian Organisation




        
    

        
        INTERSOS is an independent humanitarian organization that assists the victims of natural disasters, armed conflicts and exclusion. Its activities are based on the principles of solidarity, justice, human dignity, equality of rights and opportunities, respect for diversity and coexistence, paying special attention to the most vulnerable people.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Field Pharmacist

Job Locations: Monguno and Magumeri, Borno

Job Purpose
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	According to Project manager and Pharmacy Officer's indications ensure the pharmacy stock management according to the good storage and distribution practices.
	This includes receiving, storing and dispensing process for drugs, consumables, medical material and equipment.
	The pharmacist is also in charge of providing treatment precautions and recommendations to the patients.


Responsibilities
Data Collection and Analysis:


	Be able to provide at any given time reliable information on stock, consumption, use of medicines and health products for each project.
	Assist the pharmacist at central level in updating stock information when required
	Create/update the stock-cards of the drug/medical material/equipment received and other tools in a timely manner
	Ensure regular and appropriate use of the management document (inventory records, delivery notes, computer tools etc.) Update the global stock inventory
	Ensure the storage of the items according to good practices of storage
	Compile, in collaboration with the medical doctor and PM, a stock transfer request to the central pharmacy
	Work closely with the medical department to check, in relation to epidemiological data, the status of stocks and the consumptions and assess the appropriateness of prescriptions.


Supply Management:


	Ensure order reception by verifying and recording the quantity and quality of the drugs/medical materials/equipment received. Inform central pharmacy and supervisor in the event of non-compliance.
	Prepare order/supply request regarding the average monthly consumption, the stock level and supply delay in collaboration with the medical doctor.
	Validate the supply request with the Project manager and send the stock transfer request to the central pharmacy Update the global stock inventory.


Stock Management:


	Set up and maintain the pharmacy stock according to good storage practices (storage of drugs by categories, alphabetic order and expiry date).
	Ensure that FEFO (first expired first out) is respected.
	Implement and ensure the stock follow up by calculating the average monthly consumption and minimal stock level for the main items
	Implement stock management system for each item and update each stock card/follow up in a timely manner
	Ensure a physical inventory of the pharmacy and medical stock, including consumables and laboratory material on a monthly basis
	Inform the project manager in case of any missing/damaged/expired item
	Make sure that the main stock and the pharmacy are kept clean
	Organise effective destruction and quarantine of damaged items in collaboration with the Central pharmacist
	Ensure pharmacovigilance including the immediate report of any problem with the quality of a drug in accordance with INTERSOS pharmaceutical management procedures.


Treatment Delivery:


	Ensure all medical equipment is prepared for the day
	Deliver the treatment to the patient according to medical doctor prescription
	Explain the posology, and providing advices and precaution of use
	Prepare the drugs for the pharmacy on a weekly basis according to the needs


Position Requirements
Education:


	Bachelor’s Degree in Pharmacy (B.Pharm or Pharm.D)
	Registration with Pharmacy Council of Nigeria with a current License.


Professional experience:


	Desirable previous experience in pharmacy for at least 2 years in similar or relevant positions.


Professional requirements:


	Essential computer literacy (word, excel and internet).


Personal requirements:


	Commitment to INTERSOS principles.


Languages:


	Mission language is essential.
	Local working language desirable.


Note


	Interested candidates should complete the form and attach their CV with Cover Letter following this format “Surname_ Position you applied for”.
	ONLY applicants whose competencies meet the requirements of the position will be contacted.
	Please note: Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis.
	INTERSOS reserves the right to close the advertisement before the stated closing date if a suitable candidate is identified.
	INTERSOS is committed to achieving workforce diversity in terms of gender, nationality, and culture. Individuals from minority groups, indigenous groups, and persons with disabilities are equally encouraged to apply.
	INTERSOS has a PSEA Policy that the successful candidate will be expected to comply with and promote.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Immigration Lawyer / Case Manager",
  "description": "DTECH Centrix Plus Limited is a leading career and education consultancy company that guides individuals toward successful career paths.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Immigration Lawyer / Case Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos (Remote)
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary&nbsp;


	The Immigration Case Manager / Immigration Lawyer will oversee the processing of immigration cases, ensuring the highest standards of compliance and efficiency.
	This position entails managing a team responsible for preparing immigration petitions and applications, liaising with clients, and maintaining case workflow in accordance with legal requirements and company protocols.


Key Responsibilities
Case Management:


	Direct the case preparation process, ensuring complete accuracy of applications and adherence to all immigration laws and regulations.
	Oversee the caseload distribution and monitor the progress of each case, adjusting resources as needed to meet deadlines.
	Provide expert advice and guidance to clients on their immigration process, maintaining an open line of communication throughout their case.


Operational Excellence:


	Develop and implement operational policies tailored to the immigration services sector, driving process improvements and strategic initiatives.
	Analyze operational data and reports to monitor performance, identify bottlenecks, and implement solutions to enhance efficiency.
	Ensure operational adherence to immigration laws, regulatory standards, and ethical practices.


Team Supervision and Development:


	Lead a team of immigration professionals, fostering an environment of learning, efficiency, and excellence.
	Conduct training sessions to keep staff updated on the latest immigration laws and case management techniques.
	Evaluate team performance, providing feedback and professional development opportunities to aid career growth.


Client Relationship Management:


	Serve as the senior point of contact for high-profile clients, ensuring their needs are met and issues are resolved expediently.
	Uphold a client-centric approach, ensuring client satisfaction and building long-term relationships.


Quality Control and Compliance:


	Maintain meticulous records and documentation for all cases, ensuring readiness for any audits or compliance checks.
	Implement quality control measures to minimize errors and ensure all submissions meet applicable legal standards.


Resource Management:


	Forecast operational needs and manage the budget, optimizing the use of resources while controlling costs.
	Identify and engage with external vendors, consultants, and government agencies as needed.


Communication and Reporting:


	Provide comprehensive reports on case statuses, operational challenges, and team performance to senior management.
	Ensure clear and effective communication within the team and across departments to align with the company&rsquo;s objectives.


Qualifications


	Bachelor&#39;s or master&rsquo;s degree in Law, or a related field.
	At least 5 years of experience in immigration case management, ideally within a legal or corporate environment.
	Strong leadership capabilities and experience managing cross-functional teams.
	Exceptional attention to detail and a deep understanding of U.S. immigration procedures and regulations.
	Vast experience in other European immigration pathways.
	Proven track record in operational management, process improvement, and strategic planning.
	Excellent communication skills, with proficiency in English; additional languages are a plus.
	Competency in case management systems and standard office software.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV&nbsp;a cover letter detailing your experience, and any relevant portfolio items to:&nbsp;hr@mypathfinderconsulting.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Immigration Lawyer / Case Manager Job at DTECH Centrix Plus Limited




        
    

        
        DTECH Centrix Plus Limited is a leading career and education consultancy company that guides individuals toward successful career paths.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Immigration Lawyer / Case Manager

Job Location: Lagos (Remote)
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary 
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Immigration Case Manager / Immigration Lawyer will oversee the processing of immigration cases, ensuring the highest standards of compliance and efficiency.
	This position entails managing a team responsible for preparing immigration petitions and applications, liaising with clients, and maintaining case workflow in accordance with legal requirements and company protocols.


Key Responsibilities
Case Management:


	Direct the case preparation process, ensuring complete accuracy of applications and adherence to all immigration laws and regulations.
	Oversee the caseload distribution and monitor the progress of each case, adjusting resources as needed to meet deadlines.
	Provide expert advice and guidance to clients on their immigration process, maintaining an open line of communication throughout their case.


Operational Excellence:


	Develop and implement operational policies tailored to the immigration services sector, driving process improvements and strategic initiatives.
	Analyze operational data and reports to monitor performance, identify bottlenecks, and implement solutions to enhance efficiency.
	Ensure operational adherence to immigration laws, regulatory standards, and ethical practices.


Team Supervision and Development:


	Lead a team of immigration professionals, fostering an environment of learning, efficiency, and excellence.
	Conduct training sessions to keep staff updated on the latest immigration laws and case management techniques.
	Evaluate team performance, providing feedback and professional development opportunities to aid career growth.


Client Relationship Management:


	Serve as the senior point of contact for high-profile clients, ensuring their needs are met and issues are resolved expediently.
	Uphold a client-centric approach, ensuring client satisfaction and building long-term relationships.


Quality Control and Compliance:


	Maintain meticulous records and documentation for all cases, ensuring readiness for any audits or compliance checks.
	Implement quality control measures to minimize errors and ensure all submissions meet applicable legal standards.


Resource Management:


	Forecast operational needs and manage the budget, optimizing the use of resources while controlling costs.
	Identify and engage with external vendors, consultants, and government agencies as needed.


Communication and Reporting:


	Provide comprehensive reports on case statuses, operational challenges, and team performance to senior management.
	Ensure clear and effective communication within the team and across departments to align with the company’s objectives.


Qualifications


	Bachelor's or master’s degree in Law, or a related field.
	At least 5 years of experience in immigration case management, ideally within a legal or corporate environment.
	Strong leadership capabilities and experience managing cross-functional teams.
	Exceptional attention to detail and a deep understanding of U.S. immigration procedures and regulations.
	Vast experience in other European immigration pathways.
	Proven track record in operational management, process improvement, and strategic planning.
	Excellent communication skills, with proficiency in English; additional languages are a plus.
	Competency in case management systems and standard office software.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV a cover letter detailing your experience, and any relevant portfolio items to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Data Analyst (Reporting & Analytics)",
  "description": "Amaiden Energy Nigeria Limited (formerly Moody International Nigeria Limited) was established in 1996 as a partnership between Moody International Group and Nigerian investors. Since commencing operations in March 1997 the company continues to grow and exerts its presence in numerous Projects in the Nigeria Oil and Gas Industry.

We are recruiting to fill the position below

Job Position:&nbsp;Data Analyst (Reporting &amp; Analytics)

Job Location:&nbsp;Bonny Island, Rivers
Job Type: Contract

Job Summary


	We are seeking a highly skilled and results-driven Senior Data Analyst to support data-driven decision-making through advanced analytics, reporting, and dashboard development.
	The ideal candidate will have strong experience in data extraction, transformation, and visualization, with a solid background in working with enterprise systems (e.g., CMMS, SAP) and delivering insights that improve operational efficiency and asset performance.


Key Responsibilities


	Extract, transform, and load (ETL) data from multiple sources including CMMS platforms (e.g., SAP-PM, PACER)
	Analyze large and complex datasets to identify trends, anomalies, and opportunities for performance improvement
	Develop and maintain interactive dashboards and reports using Power BI, Excel, and other visualization tools
	Perform advanced data analysis using SQL, DAX, and Python (NumPy, Pandas)
	Monitor and ensure data quality, integrity, and consistency across systems
	Support internal and external audits by providing accurate data and reporting documentation
	Prepare and present periodic reports (e.g., Asset Integrity Reports, Operational Performance Reports)
	Collaborate with cross-functional teams (Planners, Schedulers, Technicians, and Management) to define data needs and reporting requirements
	Automate data processes and reporting workflows to improve efficiency
	Design and develop data-driven tools (Excel, Access, Power Apps) to enhance productivity
	Provide training and support to end-users on data tools, dashboards, and CMMS systems.


Requirements &amp; Qualifications


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Statistics, Mathematics, or a related field
	5 - 8 years of experience in data analysis, reporting, or business intelligence roles
	Strong experience with SQL and database management (MySQL, MS Access, MongoDB, etc.)
	Proficiency in Power BI, Microsoft Excel (advanced), and Microsoft Power Platform
	Hands-on experience with Python for data analysis (NumPy, Pandas, Jupyter Notebook)
	Familiarity with enterprise systems such as SAP-PM or other CMMS tools
	Experience in data visualization and dashboard design
	Knowledge of ETL processes and data warehousing concepts
	Strong understanding of KPIs, performance metrics, and reporting frameworks
	Excellent problem-solving, analytical, and critical thinking skills
	Strong communication and stakeholder engagement skills.
",

  "validThrough": "2026-04-24",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

Data Analyst (Reporting & Analytics) Job at Amaiden Energy Nigeria Limited




        
    

        
        Amaiden Energy Nigeria Limited (formerly Moody International Nigeria Limited) was established in 1996 as a partnership between Moody International Group and Nigerian investors. Since commencing operations in March 1997 the company continues to grow and exerts its presence in numerous Projects in the Nigeria Oil and Gas Industry.

We are recruiting to fill the position below

Job Position: Data Analyst (Reporting & Analytics)

Job Location: Bonny Island, Rivers
Job Type: Contract

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a highly skilled and results-driven Senior Data Analyst to support data-driven decision-making through advanced analytics, reporting, and dashboard development.
	The ideal candidate will have strong experience in data extraction, transformation, and visualization, with a solid background in working with enterprise systems (e.g., CMMS, SAP) and delivering insights that improve operational efficiency and asset performance.


Key Responsibilities


	Extract, transform, and load (ETL) data from multiple sources including CMMS platforms (e.g., SAP-PM, PACER)
	Analyze large and complex datasets to identify trends, anomalies, and opportunities for performance improvement
	Develop and maintain interactive dashboards and reports using Power BI, Excel, and other visualization tools
	Perform advanced data analysis using SQL, DAX, and Python (NumPy, Pandas)
	Monitor and ensure data quality, integrity, and consistency across systems
	Support internal and external audits by providing accurate data and reporting documentation
	Prepare and present periodic reports (e.g., Asset Integrity Reports, Operational Performance Reports)
	Collaborate with cross-functional teams (Planners, Schedulers, Technicians, and Management) to define data needs and reporting requirements
	Automate data processes and reporting workflows to improve efficiency
	Design and develop data-driven tools (Excel, Access, Power Apps) to enhance productivity
	Provide training and support to end-users on data tools, dashboards, and CMMS systems.


Requirements & Qualifications


	Bachelor’s Degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Statistics, Mathematics, or a related field
	5 - 8 years of experience in data analysis, reporting, or business intelligence roles
	Strong experience with SQL and database management (MySQL, MS Access, MongoDB, etc.)
	Proficiency in Power BI, Microsoft Excel (advanced), and Microsoft Power Platform
	Hands-on experience with Python for data analysis (NumPy, Pandas, Jupyter Notebook)
	Familiarity with enterprise systems such as SAP-PM or other CMMS tools
	Experience in data visualization and dashboard design
	Knowledge of ETL processes and data warehousing concepts
	Strong understanding of KPIs, performance metrics, and reporting frameworks
	Excellent problem-solving, analytical, and critical thinking skills
	Strong communication and stakeholder engagement skills.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Financial Sales Executive",
  "description": "Smart Wealth is a modern financial platform designed to help individuals take control of their money with confidence and clarity. Built with simplicity and efficiency in mind, it provides users with powerful tools to manage, grow, and monitor their financial activities&mdash;all in one secure environment.With a focus on accessibility and smart decision-making, Smart Wealth empowers users to explore new opportunities, track performance in real time, and make informed financial moves without unnecessary complexity. The platform combines intuitive design with advanced technology, ensuring a seamless experience for both beginners and experienced users.

Security, transparency, and user empowerment are at the core of Smart Wealth. Whether you&#39;re looking to build consistency in your financial journey or expand your earning potential, Smart Wealth positions you with the tools and insights needed to move forward confidently.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Financial Sales Executive

Job Location:&nbsp;Remote (Nigeria-based)
Employment Type: Full-time
Work Hours: 3:00 PM &ndash; 11:00 PM (WAT)

Job Description


	The Financial Sales Executive will be responsible for engaging potential clients, introducing them to Smart Wealth&rsquo;s financial solutions, and guiding them toward making informed financial commitments.
	This role requires strong communication skills, confidence, and the ability to build trust with international clients, particularly from the U.S.


You will be expected to:


	Make outbound calls to prospective clients
	Present and explain financial opportunities in a clear and compelling manner
	Build rapport and maintain strong client relationships
	Consistently meet or exceed sales targets
	Maintain professionalism and accuracy in all client interactions.


Training &amp; Probation:


	Training Duration: 3 days to 1 week
	Probation Period: 1 month.


Requirements


	First-Class degree or strong academic background in any discipline
	Must be comfortable speaking with American clients
	Strong sales experience (mandatory)
	Excellent spoken and written English (near-native fluency preferred)
	Previous experience working with international clients is highly preferred
	Ability to work in a quiet and distraction-free environment
	Strong persuasion, negotiation, and interpersonal skills
	Must be confident, disciplined, and target-driven.


Why Join Smart Wealth?


	Salary: N200,000 &ndash; N500,000 per month.
	Competitive salary with performance incentives
	Fully remote work structure
	Opportunity to work with international clients
	Fast-paced environment with growth potential.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their&nbsp;application along with a 1-minute voice note introducing yourself via the provided contact channel to:&nbsp;uzochukwu.chinenye23@gmail.com&nbsp;using &ldquo;Financial Sales Executive&rdquo; as the subject of the mail.",

  "validThrough": "2026-04-25",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",
  "hiringOrganization": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "Smart Wealth"
    
  },
  "jobLocation": {
    "@type": "Place",
    "address": {
      "@type": "PostalAddress",
       "postalCode": ". ",
       "addressRegion": ". ",

Financial Sales Executive Job at Smart Wealth




        
    

        
        Smart Wealth is a modern financial platform designed to help individuals take control of their money with confidence and clarity. Built with simplicity and efficiency in mind, it provides users with powerful tools to manage, grow, and monitor their financial activities—all in one secure environment.With a focus on accessibility and smart decision-making, Smart Wealth empowers users to explore new opportunities, track performance in real time, and make informed financial moves without unnecessary complexity. The platform combines intuitive design with advanced technology, ensuring a seamless experience for both beginners and experienced users.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Security, transparency, and user empowerment are at the core of Smart Wealth. Whether you're looking to build consistency in your financial journey or expand your earning potential, Smart Wealth positions you with the tools and insights needed to move forward confidently.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Financial Sales Executive

Job Location: Remote (Nigeria-based)
Employment Type: Full-time
Work Hours: 3:00 PM – 11:00 PM (WAT)

Job Description


	The Financial Sales Executive will be responsible for engaging potential clients, introducing them to Smart Wealth’s financial solutions, and guiding them toward making informed financial commitments.
	This role requires strong communication skills, confidence, and the ability to build trust with international clients, particularly from the U.S.


You will be expected to:


	Make outbound calls to prospective clients
	Present and explain financial opportunities in a clear and compelling manner
	Build rapport and maintain strong client relationships
	Consistently meet or exceed sales targets
	Maintain professionalism and accuracy in all client interactions.


Training & Probation:


	Training Duration: 3 days to 1 week
	Probation Period: 1 month.


Requirements


	First-Class degree or strong academic background in any discipline
	Must be comfortable speaking with American clients
	Strong sales experience (mandatory)
	Excellent spoken and written English (near-native fluency preferred)
	Previous experience working with international clients is highly preferred
	Ability to work in a quiet and distraction-free environment
	Strong persuasion, negotiation, and interpersonal skills
	Must be confident, disciplined, and target-driven.


Why Join Smart Wealth?


	Salary: N200,000 – N500,000 per month.
	Competitive salary with performance incentives
	Fully remote work structure
	Opportunity to work with international clients
	Fast-paced environment with growth potential.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their application along with a 1-minute voice note introducing yourself via the provided contact channel to: [email protected] using “Financial Sales Executive” as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply
